english_name: The First Epistle of Paul the Apostle to the Corinthians

verses.latin_vulgate: Sectamini caritatem, æmulamini spiritualia: magis autem ut prophetetis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διώκετε τὴν ἀγάπην ζηλοῦτε δὲ τὰ πνευματικά μᾶλλον δὲ ἵνα προφητεύητε
verses.king_james: Follow after charity, and desire spiritual *gifts*, but rather that ye may prophesy.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui enim loquitur lingua, non hominibus loquitur, sed Deo: nemo enim audit. Spiritu autem loquitur mysteria.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ γὰρ λαλῶν γλώσσῃ οὐκ ἀνθρώποις λαλεῖ ἀλλὰ τῶ θεῷ οὐδεὶς γὰρ ἀκούει πνεύματι δὲ λαλεῖ μυστήρια
verses.king_james: For he that speaketh in an *unknown* tongue speaketh not unto men, but unto God: for no man understandeth *him*; howbeit in the spirit he speaketh mysteries.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nam qui prophetat, hominibus loquitur ad ædificationem, et exhortationem, et consolationem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ προφητεύων ἀνθρώποις λαλεῖ οἰκοδομὴν καὶ παράκλησιν καὶ παραμυθίαν
verses.king_james: But he that prophesieth speaketh unto men *to* edification, and exhortation, and comfort.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui loquitur lingua, semetipsum ædificat: qui autem prophetat, ecclesiam Dei ædificat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ λαλῶν γλώσσῃ ἑαυτὸν οἰκοδομεῖ ὁ δὲ προφητεύων ἐκκλησίαν οἰκοδομεῖ
verses.king_james: He that speaketh in an *unknown* tongue edifieth himself; but he that prophesieth edifieth the church.

verses.latin_vulgate: Volo autem omnes vos loqui linguis: magis autem prophetare. Nam major est qui prophetat, quam qui loquitur linguis; nisi forte interpretetur ut ecclesia ædificationem accipiat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: θέλω δὲ πάντας ὑμᾶς λαλεῖν γλώσσαις μᾶλλον δὲ ἵνα προφητεύητε μείζων γὰρ ὁ προφητεύων ἢ ὁ λαλῶν γλώσσαις ἐκτὸς εἰ μὴ διερμηνεύῃ ἵνα ἡ ἐκκλησία οἰκοδομὴν λάβῃ
verses.king_james: I would that ye all spake with tongues, but rather that ye prophesied: for greater *is* he that prophesieth than he that speaketh with tongues, except he interpret, that the church may receive edifying.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nunc autem, fratres, si venero ad vos linguis loquens: quid vobis prodero, nisi vobis loquar aut in revelatione, aut in scientia, aut in prophetia, aut in doctrina?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: νυνὶ δέ ἀδελφοί ἐὰν ἔλθω πρὸς ὑμᾶς γλώσσαις λαλῶν τί ὑμᾶς ὠφελήσω ἐὰν μὴ ὑμῖν λαλήσω ἢ ἐν ἀποκαλύψει ἢ ἐν γνώσει ἢ ἐν προφητείᾳ ἢ ἐν διδαχῇ
verses.king_james: Now, brethren, if I come unto you speaking with tongues, what shall I profit you, except I shall speak to you either by revelation, or by knowledge, or by prophesying, or by doctrine?

verses.latin_vulgate: Tamen quæ sine anima sunt vocem dantia, sive tibia, sive cithara; nisi distinctionem sonituum dederint, quomodo scietur id quod canitur, aut quod citharizatur?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅμως τὰ ἄψυχα φωνὴν διδόντα εἴτε αὐλὸς εἴτε κιθάρα ἐὰν διαστολὴν τοῖς φθόγγοις μὴ δῷ πῶς γνωσθήσεται τὸ αὐλούμενον ἢ τὸ κιθαριζόμενον
verses.king_james: And even things without life giving sound, whether pipe or harp, except they give a distinction in the sounds, how shall it be known what is piped or harped?

verses.latin_vulgate: Etenim si incertam vocem det tuba, quis parabit se ad bellum?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ γὰρ ἐὰν ἄδηλον φωνὴν σάλπιγξ δῷ τίς παρασκευάσεται εἰς πόλεμον
verses.king_james: For if the trumpet give an uncertain sound, who shall prepare himself to the battle?

verses.latin_vulgate: Ita et vos per linguam nisi manifestum sermonem dederitis: quomodo scietur id quod dicitur? eritis enim in aëra loquentes.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὕτως καὶ ὑμεῖς διὰ τῆς γλώσσης ἐὰν μὴ εὔσημον λόγον δῶτε πῶς γνωσθήσεται τὸ λαλούμενον ἔσεσθε γὰρ εἰς ἀέρα λαλοῦντες
verses.king_james: So likewise ye, except ye utter by the tongue words easy to be understood, how shall it be known what is spoken? for ye shall speak into the air.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tam multa, ut puta genera linguarum sunt in hoc mundo: et nihil sine voce est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοσαῦτα εἰ τύχοι γένη φωνῶν ἐστιν ἐν κόσμῳ καὶ οὐδὲν αὐτῶν ἄφωνον
verses.king_james: There are, it may be, so many kinds of voices in the world, and none of them *is* without signification.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si ergo nesciero virtutem vocis, ero ei, cui loquor, barbarus: et qui loquitur, mihi barbarus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐὰν οὖν μὴ εἰδῶ τὴν δύναμιν τῆς φωνῆς ἔσομαι τῷ λαλοῦντι βάρβαρος καὶ ὁ λαλῶν ἐν ἐμοὶ βάρβαρος
verses.king_james: Therefore if I know not the meaning of the voice, I shall be unto him that speaketh a barbarian, and he that speaketh *shall be* a barbarian unto me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sic et vos, quoniam æmulatores estis spirituum, ad ædificationem ecclesiæ quærite ut abundetis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὕτως καὶ ὑμεῖς ἐπεὶ ζηλωταί ἐστε πνευμάτων πρὸς τὴν οἰκοδομὴν τῆς ἐκκλησίας ζητεῖτε ἵνα περισσεύητε
verses.king_james: Even so ye, forasmuch as ye are zealous of spiritual *gifts*, seek that ye may excel to the edifying of the church.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ideo qui loquitur lingua, oret ut interpretetur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διόπερ ὁ λαλῶν γλώσσῃ προσευχέσθω ἵνα διερμηνεύῃ
verses.king_james: Wherefore let him that speaketh in an *unknown* tongue pray that he may interpret.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nam si orem lingua, spiritus meus orat, mens autem mea sine fructu est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐὰν γὰρ προσεύχωμαι γλώσσῃ τὸ πνεῦμά μου προσεύχεται ὁ δὲ νοῦς μου ἄκαρπός ἐστιν
verses.king_james: For if I pray in an *unknown* tongue, my spirit prayeth, but my understanding is unfruitful.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quid ergo est? Orabo spiritu, orabo et mente: psallam spiritu, psallam et mente.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τί οὖν ἐστιν προσεύξομαι τῷ πνεύματι προσεύξομαι δὲ καὶ τῷ νοΐ ψαλῶ τῷ πνεύματι ψαλῶ δὲ καὶ τῷ νοΐ
verses.king_james: What is it then? I will pray with the spirit, and I will pray with the understanding also: I will sing with the spirit, and I will sing with the understanding also.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ceterum si benedixeris spiritu, qui supplet locum idiotæ, quomodo dicet: Amen, super tuam benedictionem? quoniam quid dicas, nescit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐπεὶ ἐὰν εὐλογήσῃς τῷ πνεύματι ὁ ἀναπληρῶν τὸν τόπον τοῦ ἰδιώτου πῶς ἐρεῖ τὸ ἀμήν ἐπὶ τῇ σῇ εὐχαριστίᾳ ἐπειδὴ τί λέγεις οὐκ οἶδεν
verses.king_james: Else when thou shalt bless with the spirit, how shall he that occupieth the room of the unlearned say Amen at thy giving of thanks, seeing he understandeth not what thou sayest?

verses.latin_vulgate: Nam tu quidem bene gratias agis, sed alter non ædificatur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: σὺ μὲν γὰρ καλῶς εὐχαριστεῖς ἀλλ᾽ ὁ ἕτερος οὐκ οἰκοδομεῖται
verses.king_james: For thou verily givest thanks well, but the other is not edified.

verses.latin_vulgate: Gratias ago Deo meo, quod omnium vestrum lingua loquor.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εὐχαριστῶ τῷ θεῷ μου, πάντων ὑμῶν μᾶλλον γλώσσαις λαλῶν
verses.king_james: I thank my God, I speak with tongues more than ye all:

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed in ecclesia volo quinque verba sensu meo loqui, ut et alios instruam: quam decem millia verborum in lingua.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλ᾽ ἐν ἐκκλησίᾳ θέλω πέντε λόγους διὰ τοῦ νοός μου λαλῆσαι ἵνα καὶ ἄλλους κατηχήσω ἢ μυρίους λόγους ἐν γλώσσῃ
verses.king_james: Yet in the church I had rather speak five words with my understanding, that *by my voice* I might teach others also, than ten thousand words in an *unknown* tongue.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fratres, nolite pueri effici sensibus, sed malitia parvuli estote: sensibus autem perfecti estote.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀδελφοί μὴ παιδία γίνεσθε ταῖς φρεσίν ἀλλὰ τῇ κακίᾳ νηπιάζετε ταῖς δὲ φρεσὶν τέλειοι γίνεσθε
verses.king_james: Brethren, be not children in understanding: howbeit in malice be ye children, but in understanding be men.

verses.latin_vulgate: In lege scriptum est: Quoniam in aliis linguis et labiis aliis loquar populo huic: et nec sic exaudient me, dicit Dominus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν τῷ νόμῳ γέγραπται ὅτι ἐν ἑτερογλώσσοις καὶ ἐν χείλεσιν ἑτέροις λαλήσω τῷ λαῷ τούτῳ καὶ οὐδ᾽ οὕτως εἰσακούσονταί μου λέγει κύριος
verses.king_james: In the law it is written, With *men of* other tongues and other lips will I speak unto this people; and yet for all that will they not hear me, saith the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Itaque linguæ in signum sunt non fidelibus, sed infidelibus: prophetiæ autem non infidelibus, sed fidelibus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὥστε αἱ γλῶσσαι εἰς σημεῖόν εἰσιν οὐ τοῖς πιστεύουσιν ἀλλὰ τοῖς ἀπίστοις ἡ δὲ προφητεία οὐ τοῖς ἀπίστοις ἀλλὰ τοῖς πιστεύουσιν
verses.king_james: Wherefore tongues are for a sign, not to them that believe, but to them that believe not: but prophesying *serveth* not for them that believe not, but for them which believe.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si ergo conveniat universa ecclesia in unum, et omnes linguis loquantur, intrent autem idiotæ, aut infideles: nonne dicent quod insanitis?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐὰν οὖν συνέλθῃ ἡ ἐκκλησία ὅλη ἐπὶ τὸ αὐτὸ καὶ πάντες γλώσσαις λαλῶσιν εἰσέλθωσιν δὲ ἰδιῶται ἢ ἄπιστοι οὐκ ἐροῦσιν ὅτι μαίνεσθε
verses.king_james: If therefore the whole church be come together into one place, and all speak with tongues, and there come in *those that are* unlearned, or unbelievers, will they not say that ye are mad?

verses.latin_vulgate: Si autem omnes prophetent, intret autem quis infidelis, vel idiota, convincitur ab omnibus, dijudicatur ab omnibus:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐὰν δὲ πάντες προφητεύωσιν εἰσέλθῃ δέ τις ἄπιστος ἢ ἰδιώτης ἐλέγχεται ὑπὸ πάντων ἀνακρίνεται ὑπὸ πάντων
verses.king_james: But if all prophesy, and there come in one that believeth not, or *one* unlearned, he is convinced of all, he is judged of all:

verses.latin_vulgate: occulta cordis ejus manifesta fiunt: et ita cadens in faciem adorabit Deum, pronuntians quod vere Deus in vobis sit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ οὕτως τὰ κρυπτὰ τῆς καρδίας αὐτοῦ φανερὰ γίνεται καὶ οὕτως πεσὼν ἐπὶ πρόσωπον προσκυνήσει τῷ θεῷ ἀπαγγέλλων ὅτι ὁ θεὸς ὄντως ἐν ὑμῖν ἐστιν
verses.king_james: And thus are the secrets of his heart made manifest; and so falling down on *his* face he will worship God, and report that God is in you of a truth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quid ergo est, fratres? Cum convenitis, unusquisque vestrum psalmum habet, doctrinam habet, apocalypsim habet, linguam habet, interpretationem habet: omnia ad ædificationem fiant.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τί οὖν ἐστιν ἀδελφοί ὅταν συνέρχησθε ἕκαστος ὑμῶν ψαλμὸν ἔχει διδαχὴν ἔχει γλῶσσαν ἔχει ἀποκάλυψιν ἔχει ἑρμηνείαν ἔχει πάντα πρὸς οἰκοδομὴν γενέσθω
verses.king_james: How is it then, brethren? when ye come together, every one of you hath a psalm, hath a doctrine, hath a tongue, hath a revelation, hath an interpretation. Let all things be done unto edifying.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sive lingua quis loquitur, secundum duos, aut ut multum tres, et per partes, et unus interpretatur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἴτε γλώσσῃ τις λαλεῖ κατὰ δύο ἢ τὸ πλεῖστον τρεῖς καὶ ἀνὰ μέρος καὶ εἷς διερμηνευέτω
verses.king_james: If any man speak in an *unknown* tongue, *let it be* by two, or at the most *by* hree, and *that* by course; and let one interpret.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si autem non fuerit interpres, taceat in ecclesia: sibi autem loquatur, et Deo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐὰν δὲ μὴ ᾖ διερμηνευτής σιγάτω ἐν ἐκκλησίᾳ ἑαυτῷ δὲ λαλείτω καὶ τῷ θεῷ
verses.king_james: But if there be no interpreter, let him keep silence in the church; and let him speak to himself, and to God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Prophetæ autem duo, aut tres dicant, et ceteri dijudicent.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: προφῆται δὲ δύο ἢ τρεῖς λαλείτωσαν καὶ οἱ ἄλλοι διακρινέτωσαν
verses.king_james: Let the prophets speak two or three, and let the other judge.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quod si alii revelatum fuerit sedenti, prior taceat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐὰν δὲ ἄλλῳ ἀποκαλυφθῇ καθημένῳ ὁ πρῶτος σιγάτω
verses.king_james: If *any thing* be revealed to another that sitteth by, let the first hold his peace.

verses.latin_vulgate: Potestis enim omnes per singulos prophetare: ut omnes discant, et omnes exhortentur:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: δύνασθε γὰρ καθ᾽ ἕνα πάντες προφητεύειν ἵνα πάντες μανθάνωσιν καὶ πάντες παρακαλῶνται
verses.king_james: For ye may all prophesy one by one, that all may learn, and all may be comforted.

verses.latin_vulgate: et spiritus prophetarum prophetis subjecti sunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ πνεύματα προφητῶν προφήταις ὑποτάσσεται
verses.king_james: And the spirits of the prophets are subject to the prophets.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non enim est dissensionis Deus, sed pacis: sicut et in omnibus ecclesiis sanctorum doceo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐ γάρ ἐστιν ἀκαταστασίας ὁ θεὸς ἀλλ᾽ εἰρήνης Ὡς ἐν πάσαις ταῖς ἐκκλησίαις τῶν ἁγίων
verses.king_james: For God is not *the author* of confusion, but of peace, as in all churches of the saints.

verses.latin_vulgate: Mulieres in ecclesiis taceant, non enim permittitur eis loqui, sed subditas esse, sicut et lex dicit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: αἱ γυναῖκες ὑμῶν ἐν ταῖς ἐκκλησίαις σιγάτωσαν οὐ γὰρ ἐπιτέτραπται αὐταῖς λαλεῖν ἀλλ᾽ ὑποτάσσεσθαι, καθὼς καὶ ὁ νόμος λέγει
verses.king_james: Let your women keep silence in the churches: for it is not permitted unto them to speak; but *they are commanded* to be under obedience, as also saith the law.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si quid autem volunt discere, domi viros suos interrogent. Turpe est enim mulieri loqui in ecclesia.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ δέ τι μαθεῖν θέλουσιν ἐν οἴκῳ τοὺς ἰδίους ἄνδρας ἐπερωτάτωσαν αἰσχρὸν γάρ ἐστιν γυναιξὶν ἐν ἐκκλησίᾳ λαλεῖν
verses.king_james: And if they will learn any thing, let them ask their husbands at home: for it is a shame for women to speak in the church.

verses.latin_vulgate: An a vobis verbum Dei processit? aut in vos solos pervenit?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἢ ἀφ᾽ ὑμῶν ὁ λόγος τοῦ θεοῦ ἐξῆλθεν ἢ εἰς ὑμᾶς μόνους κατήντησεν
verses.king_james: What? came the word of God out from you? or came it unto you only?

verses.latin_vulgate: Si quis videtur propheta esse, aut spiritualis, cognoscat quæ scribo vobis, quia Domini sunt mandata.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἴ τις δοκεῖ προφήτης εἶναι ἢ πνευματικός ἐπιγινωσκέτω ἃ γράφω ὑμῖν ὅτι τοῦ κυρίου εἰσὶν ἐντολαί
verses.king_james: If any man think himself to be a prophet, or spiritual, let him acknowledge that the things that I write unto you are the commandments of the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si quis autem ignorat, ignorabitur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ δέ τις ἀγνοεῖ ἀγνοέιτω
verses.king_james: But if any man be ignorant, let him be ignorant.

verses.latin_vulgate: Itaque fratres æmulamini prophetare: et loqui linguis nolite prohibere.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὥστε ἀδελφοί ζηλοῦτε τὸ προφητεύειν καὶ τὸ λαλεῖν γλώσσαις μὴ κωλύετε
verses.king_james: Wherefore, brethren, covet to prophesy, and forbid not to speak with tongues.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnia autem honeste, et secundum ordinem fiant.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πάντα εὐσχημόνως καὶ κατὰ τάξιν γινέσθω
verses.king_james: Let all things be done decently and in order.
